name: python-fastapi-postgres
description: Async REST API scaffold with SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic migrations, Docker Compose for local Postgres, pytest with async fixtures, and GitHub Actions CI. Production-shape starting point for backend services.
included: FastAPI app with lifespan hooks and example Item CRUD routes
GitHub Actions CI: ruff check + pytest
selection_hints: Python is explicitly chosen or default
Needs PostgreSQL or a SQL database
Async I/O is desirable or the brief mentions scale
anti_hints: Non-Python language explicitly requested (Rust, Go, Java, TypeScript, …)
Document or event store instead of relational (MongoDB, DynamoDB, Kafka-first)
Serverless-first architecture where Lambda handlers replace a long-lived service
Data pipeline / batch job rather than an HTTP API

verification.description: Runs `ruff check .` for lint and `pytest -q` for the smoke test. Exit 0 means the customised scaffold boots, imports cleanly, and the /health endpoint returns 200.

primary_files: README.md — replace project description with what the brief actually builds
app/models.py — define ORM models for your domain entities (the Item model is a placeholder, remove or replace)
app/api/routes.py — add your endpoints; the example /items routes are a pattern, not a requirement
app/config.py — add any new settings fields you need (database_url and env are already wired)
tests/test_items.py — replace with tests for your real endpoints
pyproject.toml — update project name and description; add runtime dependencies as needed
preserve: Dockerfile multi-stage structure and non-root USER line
docker-compose.yml service topology (unless adding a new service like Redis)
alembic.ini and migrations/env.py — already wired to app.models.Base
